description: Re-scoped after the typed-depth-exhaustiveness PRD was proven architecturally infeasible (the depth stage runs on lowered Go via importer.Default(); the unresolved-* deferrals are exactly the cases that don't lower and that importer can't see — verified on-disk, archived to history/prd-typed-depth-exhaustiveness-INFEASIBLE.json). This PRD delivers the corrective that the whole detour proved necessary: a single, empirically-VERIFIED authoritative statement of the language's current state, replacing the stale token-splice-era claims scattered in ROADMAP_TO_GOAL.md / TODO.md that caused the mis-scope. SHARED FACTS: (1) Every status claim in the new doc must be backed by an actual `go run ./cmd/goalc <probe>.goal` result or a cited source file:line — no claims from the historical docs taken on faith (those predate the AST rewrite). (2) docs/*.md that are NOT verified against the toolchain (i.e. anything other than docs/by-example.md / docs/overview.md, which the playground live-verifies) do not trip verify-generated — so STATUS.md and pointer edits are safe. (3) verifyCommands (`task check`, `task fixpoint`) still gate every story and must stay green; doc-only changes do not affect the Go build, so this confirms nothing regressed. (4) Commit via the /commit-message skill; append to progress.txt per story.

userStories.title: Write a verified language-state document
userStories.description: As a maintainer (or future planner), I need one authoritative, empirically-verified statement of what the goal language actually supports today, so I never again scope work against stale token-splice-era docs.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/STATUS.md exists with dated sections: (a) Complete / working — with at least the verified facts that stored Result/Option (struct field, param, slice), closed-E `?` across matching enums, and derive over resolvable structs all lower clean; (b) Deferred to real types (why the depth stage cannot close them) — cross-file/out-of-package match exhaustiveness, derive field convertibility recursion; (c) Deliberate design boundaries (located diagnostics) — `?` only on assignment RHS, multi-value/complex `?` LHS; (d) Genuinely open (needs a decision) — inline `?` in expression position (today an ICE: `backend: unsupported expression *ast.UnwrapExpr`), nested `Result.Err(variant)` patterns, value-position `x := match` inference, the importer.Default()->ForCompiler(source) swap.

Each claim in section (a)/(d) cites either a probe command + observed result or a source file:line (e.g. internal/typecheck/typecheck.goal:106 for importer.Default; internal/backend/emit.goal for the match/`?` lowering sites).
`grep -q 'importer.Default' docs/STATUS.md && grep -q 'unsupported expression' docs/STATUS.md && grep -q 'design boundar' docs/STATUS.md` succeeds (the key verified anchors are present).
`task check` and `task fixpoint` pass unchanged (doc-only change breaks nothing).
userStories.notes: Ground every claim in re-run probes; do not copy from ROADMAP_TO_GOAL.md/TODO.md (stale, token-splice era). Verified anchors already established this session: importer.Default() at typecheck.goal:106 (+ DECISIONS.md:1743-1749 flags the ForCompiler('source') swap as OPEN); inline `?` -> `backend: unsupported expression *ast.UnwrapExpr`; stored Result/Option + closed-E ? + resolvable derive all exit 0; value-position `x := match` needs inferable arm type (emit.goal:1860); `?`-assignment-form boundaries are located codes question-assign-unsupported/question-binds-nonvalue (catalog.goal:40,44). Keep it a concise reference, not a narrative.

userStories.title: Point stale planning docs at STATUS.md
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need the historical roadmap/TODO to send readers to the verified current state, so the stale documents can no longer be mistaken for the live picture.
acceptanceCriteria: ROADMAP_TO_GOAL.md and TODO.md each gain a one-line pointer at the top referencing docs/STATUS.md as the authoritative current state (`grep -q 'STATUS.md' ROADMAP_TO_GOAL.md && grep -q 'STATUS.md' TODO.md`).

The most concretely-wrong stale premise is corrected in place: ROADMAP_TO_GOAL.md's 'No new parser for goal' / lexer-only claim (§1.3, §3) is annotated as superseded by the AST front-end rewrite (internal/lexer/parser/sema/backend), since that claim now actively misleads.
README.md's status/architecture section references docs/STATUS.md (`grep -q 'STATUS.md' README.md`).
userStories.notes: ROADMAP_TO_GOAL.md and TODO.md already carry 'Historical' banners; this adds the forward pointer + fixes the one premise that is not just stale but inverted (the rewrite added a real parser/AST, contradicting 'no new parser'). Pointer edits only — do not rewrite the historical bodies. README may already have an architecture section; append the pointer there.
